summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #552 where the initial provider setup model selector only allowed single-model selection, add multi-select support to match the existing preferred models flow.
summary.outcome: Converted showModelSelector() to multi-select with toggle, Select All, and explicit Continue button; updated saveAndFinishProvider() to accept model arrays and save via providers.save_models RPC; committed, pushed, created PR #557, addressed two review comments, and committed fixes.

repo: A multi-select modal (showMultiModelSelector, line 1124) already existed for the 'Preferred Models' button on configured providers — the initial setup just didn't use the same pattern.

The backend already supported multiple models: providers.save_models RPC accepts Vec<String>, ProviderConfig.models is Vec<String>, and normalize_model_list() handles deduplication — no Rust changes were needed.
The beads issue tracker uses a Dolt SQL server that can go down; issues are tracked there and linked to GitHub issues.
Tailwind CSS is pre-compiled/minified; classes used by showMultiModelSelector (shrink-0, min-h-0, flex-1) were already in the compiled CSS bundle, so no rebuild was needed.
The onboarding view has its own separate model selector flow, distinct from the provider modal showModelSelector — changes to one don't affect the other.
E2E oauth tests use /Select Model/i regex which matches 'Select Models' as a substring, so renaming the heading didn't break tests.

code.finding: showModelSelector() — original single-click-to-save flow; converted to multi-select toggle with Select All button, status count, and explicit Continue button.
saveAndFinishProvider() — updated to accept array of model IDs, tests only first model for connectivity, saves all via providers.save_models RPC.
Caller passes null for BYOM providers that already have a model specified — handled by selectedModelIds ? [selectedModelIds] : [] fallback yielding empty array, preserving prior behavior.
showMultiModelSelector() — the existing multi-select pattern for preferred models that served as the template for the fix.
Uses /Select Model/i regex — substring match means it still passes after heading renamed to 'Select Models'.

workflow: Biome is used for JS linting and formatting; run after edits to catch issues before commit.
The project uses worktrees (superset/worktrees/moltis/<branch-name>) for feature branches.
PR review comments are fetched via gh CLI to address them programmatically.
PR conversations are resolved via gh CLI after pushing fixes.

friction: Dolt/beads database server was down during initial issue creation attempts, requiring multiple retries and workaround (proceed with implementation first, retry later).
Three separate retry attempts to create the beads issue before the server recovered.
Had to manually attempt to repair and restart the Dolt server before beads became available.
open_items: The beads issue was created and linked but the PR #557 review fix commit was not explicitly linked back to close the beads issue — worth verifying the issue is properly closed.
Only the first selected model is tested for connectivity during setup — could consider surfacing errors if a non-first model fails later, though this was an intentional scoping decision.
No new E2E tests were written for the multi-select flow in the provider setup modal — existing tests only cover the header text match.
